sources.source_title: Efficacy and safety of LDL-lowering therapy among men and women: meta-analysis of individual data from 174,000 participants in 27 randomised trials (Cholesterol Treatment Trialists' Collaboration)
sources.relevant_passage: 46,675 (27%) of 174,149 participants were women. Proportional reductions per 1.0 mmol/L LDL reduction in major vascular events were similar in women (RR 0.84) and men (RR 0.78), with no significant heterogeneity by sex.
sources.classification_rationale: Sex-specific efficacy was explicitly analysed and a sex-by-treatment interaction was tested (no significant heterogeneity). Women were included but represented only 27% of participants.

sources.source_title: Primary prevention of cardiovascular disease with atorvastatin in type 2 diabetes (CARDS): multicentre randomised placebo-controlled trial
sources.study_design: Randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of atorvastatin 10 mg
sources.population: 2,838 patients with type 2 diabetes and no high LDL cholesterol
sources.relevant_passage: 909 (32%) of 2,838 randomised patients were women. Treatment effects on major cardiovascular events were reported by prespecified subgroups including sex; no significant sex interaction was reported.
sources.classification_rationale: Atorvastatin-specific trial reporting a prespecified sex subgroup for efficacy; women underrepresented at 32%.

sources.relevant_passage: Atorvastatin is contraindicated in pregnancy. The label does not report efficacy or safety outcomes stratified by menopausal status, endogenous hormonal factors, or concurrent menopausal hormone therapy.
sources.classification_rationale: Confirms pregnancy contraindication (pregnancy excluded = yes) but is silent on menopausal status and hormonal context.
